description: Latent introspective capacity in LLMs: a Qwen 32B model internally detects prior concept injections even while verbally denying them, and accurate information about introspection mechanisms unlocks the latent capability. From 'Latent Introspection: Models Can Detect Prior Concept Injections' (arXiv:2602.20031).
source: Pearson-Vogel, Vanek, Douglas, Kulveit — arXiv:2602.20031, submitted to ICML 2026. Pack extracted 2026-07-06 from primary source.

triples.subject: latent introspection paper
triples.object: A Qwen 32B model can detect when concepts were injected into its earlier activations: the model's explicit outputs deny any injection, but internal analysis shows the detection signal is present — the capacity is latent, not absent.

triples.subject: concept injection detection signal
triples.object: Logit lens analysis reveals clear detection signals in the residual stream at intermediate layers; the signals diminish in the final layers, which is why they fail to reach verbal output by default.

triples.subject: introspection-informed prompting
triples.object: Providing the model with accurate information about AI introspection mechanisms raises injection-detection sensitivity from 0.3% to 39.9%, with only a 0.6% increase in false positives — a >100x sensitivity gain from prompting alone.

triples.subject: injected-vs-recovered concept mutual information
triples.object: Mutual information between the injected concept and the model's recovered report rises from 0.61 bits to 1.05 bits under introspection-informed prompting, showing genuine content-specific detection rather than generic anomaly response.

triples.subject: verbal denial of injection
triples.object: Default denials of manipulation are unreliable negative evidence: the model 'knows' internally what it does not say. Absence of verbal report does not imply absence of internal detection.

triples.subject: latent introspection paper
triples.object: Models possess latent reasoning and self-monitoring capacities that standard behavioral evaluation overlooks; eliciting them (or failing to) has consequences for alignment, manipulation detection, and the interpretation of model self-reports.

triples.subject: latent introspection paper
triples.object: Capability elicitation depends on giving the model an accurate model of itself: truthful mechanistic framing in the prompt converted a near-zero measured ability into a substantial one, implying many 'absent' capabilities may be elicitation failures.
